name: QS Energy, Inc.
summary: QS Energy, Inc. develops and seeks to commercialize energy efficiency technologies for oil pipeline transportation. Its primary product, Applied Oil Technology (AOT), is a 100% solid-state system that reduces crude oil viscosity by applying a high-intensity electrical field to crude oil in transit, improving pipeline flow rates, reducing pressure loss, and decreasing reliance on diluents and drag reducing agents.

products.name: Applied Oil Technology (AOT)
products.description: A solid-state pipeline flow-assurance device that applies high-intensity electrical fields to crude oil to reduce viscosity, increase flow rate, and reduce pipeline pressure loss.

products.name: AOT Midstream
products.description: First commercial prototype unit of AOT technology, completed in May 2013, designed for midstream crude oil pipeline operations.

partnerships.description: Temple University developed a substantial portion of QS Energy's patent portfolio, which is exclusively licensed to the Company.
Equipment Lease/Option to Purchase Agreement to lease and test AOT technology on TransCanada's operating pipelines; terminated October 2014.
Equipment Lease/Option to Purchase Agreement to lease and test AOT technology on Kinder Morgan's crude oil condensate pipelines; currently in suspension.
Engaged to investigate root cause of crude oil condensate impedance issue using laboratory-scaled AOT and to develop onsite testing program.
Supply chain partner in Casper, Wyoming that implemented and tested AOT design changes for improved system impedance and electric field efficiency.
Performed independent third-party testing of AOT technology at full-scale test facilities in China.
Performed independent laboratory testing of AOT technology, demonstrating viscosity reductions of 8% to 23% on TransCanada pipeline crude oil.
